reasoning: The Metaculus community estimate was 18% on July 24, leaving 39 days until the September 1 deadline. Ten days have passed peacefully since then, leaving only 28 days for a strike to occur. Applying a simple constant-hazard extrapolation from the community's 18% forecast yields a baseline probability of roughly 13% for the remaining period. Qualitatively, news reports indicate that the US and European powers have set a late-August deadline for a new nuclear deal, with snapback sanctions threatened if no agreement is reached. Because diplomatic negotiations will be ongoing throughout August, it is highly unlikely that Israel would launch an unprovoked strike during this window, as it would severely undermine US efforts and anger the Trump administration, which mediated the June 24 ceasefire. Any military escalation resulting from failed talks or Iran leaving the NPT would almost certainly occur in September or later. Thus, the risk of an attack before September 1 is lower than a flat baseline suggests. I estimate the probability at 11%.
